content.problemStatement:
**This shares part of the initial setup with Good Ranking (Pair)**.

There are NNN contestants numbered 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N. Your task is to make them participate in some contests  and then manipulate the ranking in a particular way. In each competition, the ranking of the contestants can be considered to be a permutation of the integers [1,N][1, N][1,N] (i.e. there are no ties).

A contestant aaa is said to beat contestant bbb in a particular contest if aaa occurs earlier than bbb in the permutation that denotes the contest ranking.

The following condition should hold:


For every triplet (a,b,c)(a, b, c)(a,b,c) such that 1≤a,b,c≤N1 \le a, b, c \le N1≤a,b,c≤N and a≠b,b≠c,c≠aa \ne b, b \ne c, c \ne aa=b,b=c,c=a, there exists at least one contest such that simultaneously, aaa beats bbb and bbb beats ccc.
Your task is to choose the number of rankings, let's say MMM and then print MMM permutations, the rankings of each of the contests. You can have at most 404040 contests, so ensure 1≤M≤401 \le M \le 401≤M≤40.


Input Format

The only line of input contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
First, output M(1≤M≤40)M (1 \le M \le 40)M(1≤M≤40) - the number of contests, and then output MMM permutations of the integers in [1,N][1, N][1,N], each on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
We have 666 contests, and print all the possible permutations of [1,3][1, 3][1,3], so we are guaranteed to satisfy the constraints.
